sourceMovie.title: Knives Out
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Knives Out (2019) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Rian Johnson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Clue
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Knives Out clicked for you, Clue (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Knives Out?
faq.answer: Try Clue, See How They Run, The Thursday Murder Club—each shares DNA with Knives Out while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Knives Out?
faq.answer: Knives Out (2019) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Knives Out part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Knives Out Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
